_comment: Hand-curated layer of docs/capabilities.json (capability-config.md §2 p.3). The emitter (stapel_chat/_capabilities.py) merges this over the derived axes. Chat exposes three CTO-facing config axes — CHAT_KINDS, ATTACHMENTS, MAX_BODY_LENGTH — curated below; SCOPE_PROVIDER is an extension seam. Keep language business-facing — it surfaces in CTO conversations, not in code.
provides: Conversations and messaging: direct (1:1, idempotent by participant pair), group and support threads on one model; text/system messages with a monotonic per-conversation seq, replies and opaque CDN attachment keys; per-participant read markers and unread counts; anchor-paginated history (canonical anchor=seq) and conversation lists; a support layer (unassigned queue, first-come assignment, open/pending/resolved with reopen); and an optional Channels consumer for realtime delivery with seq-replay resume.

requires.reason: comm bus (chat.message / chat.support.assigned emits via the outbox), JWT authentication (HTTP + optional Channels), AppSettings config layer, anchor pagination
every endpoint requires an authenticated user (IsAuthenticated); stapel-auth is the shelf's session issuer — any stapel-core-compatible JWT issuer satisfies the check
messages carry opaque attachment keys only; a file/CDN module (or any host storage) owns the bytes those keys point at

extension_points.name: SCOPE_PROVIDER
extension_points.summary: Swap how the opaque scope_key (workspace/org/tenant) is resolved from the request and enforced on querysets — the default is a single global scope; a host may return the active workspace_id so conversations are partitioned per tenant.

extension_points.name: chat.message
extension_points.summary: Emitted (transactionally, via the outbox) whenever a message is appended. Realtime delivery, search indexing and notifications subscribe without any coupling in the engine.

extension_points.name: chat.support.assigned
extension_points.summary: Emitted when a support conversation is claimed by an operator — routing and operator-notification layers subscribe.

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every view declares request/response serializer seams (SerializerSeamMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, remount the URL.

CHAT_KINDS.summary: The enabled subset of direct (1:1), group and support threads. Drop "support" to run a pure peer-to-peer chat with no operator queue or assignment; drop "group" to allow only 1:1 direct threads. Creating (or, for support, operating on) a kind that is not enabled is refused. Values: any non-empty subset of direct, group, support.
CHAT_KINDS.behavior: A create/operate request for a kind not in CHAT_KINDS is rejected (400); the support queue/assign/resolve/reopen endpoints answer 400 when "support" is not enabled.

ATTACHMENTS.summary: When on, a message may carry a list of opaque attachment keys (the files live in the host's CDN/storage; the module stores only the keys). When off, any message with an attachment is rejected. Text is always allowed.
ATTACHMENTS.behavior: off: a message with a non-empty attachments list is rejected (400); on: attachment keys are accepted and stored on the message.

MAX_BODY_LENGTH.summary: Hard cap, in characters, on a single text message body. A longer body is rejected before it is stored. Raise it for long-form support replies, lower it for terse chat.
MAX_BODY_LENGTH.behavior: A body longer than MAX_BODY_LENGTH characters is rejected (400) at the API boundary.
